title: Proverbs 3:5-6
message: Trust in the LORD with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.
notes: The condition is total trust, not partial. When I submit all my ways — not just the spiritual ones — He takes responsibility for the path.

title: Psalm 32:8
message: I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you with my loving eye on you.
notes: God speaks this in first person. He is not a distant guide — He is personally watching, teaching, and counseling me every step.

title: Isaiah 30:21
message: Whether you turn to the right or to the left, your ears will hear a voice behind you, saying, 'This is the way; walk in it.'
notes: He speaks at the point of decision — not before or after, but exactly when I need to hear it. His direction is always timely.

title: John 16:13
message: But when he, the Spirit of truth, comes, he will guide you into all the truth. He will not speak on his own; he will speak only what he hears, and he will tell you what is yet to come.
notes: The Holy Spirit guides me into all truth — not some. His counsel is not partial. He also tells me what is to come, so I walk into the future with insider knowledge.

title: Proverbs 16:9
message: In their heart humans plan their course, but the LORD establishes their steps.
notes: Planning is not wrong — but God has final say over my steps. I bring my intentions; He establishes the actual path.

title: Psalm 37:23
message: The LORD makes firm the steps of the one who delights in him.
notes: He makes my steps firm — not tentative, not shaky. Delight in Him is the foundation that steadies every move I make.

title: Isaiah 58:11
message: The LORD will guide you always; he will satisfy your needs in a sun-scorched land and will strengthen your frame. You will be like a well-watered garden, like a spring whose waters never fail.
notes: His guidance comes with provision. He doesn't just show me the path — He sustains me on it. Even in dry seasons, I am a well-watered garden.

title: James 1:5
message: If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you.
notes: God gives wisdom generously and without fault-finding. He does not make me feel foolish for asking. I can ask freely and expect a full answer.

title: Psalm 25:9
message: He guides the humble in what is right and teaches the humble his way.
notes: Humility is the posture that opens the channel for direction. When I stop insisting on my own way, He begins to show me His.

title: Psalm 73:24
message: You guide me with your counsel, and afterward you will take me into glory.
notes: His counsel carries me all the way through — from here to glory. My entire life's journey is held within His guidance.

title: Proverbs 20:24
message: A person's steps are directed by the LORD. How then can anyone understand their own way?
notes: I do not need to have my whole path figured out. God directs each step. My job is the next one, and He handles the rest.

title: Romans 8:14
message: For those who are led by the Spirit of God are the children of God.
notes: Being led by the Spirit is a mark of sonship. I was born to be guided by God — it is central to who I am as His child.

title: Psalm 48:14
message: For this God is our God for ever and ever; he will be our guide even to the end.
notes: He is my guide to the very end — not just the beginning or the middle. There is no season of my life where His guidance runs out.

title: Isaiah 42:16
message: I will lead the blind by ways they have not known, along unfamiliar paths I will guide them; I will turn the darkness into light before them and make the rough places smooth. These are the things I will do; I will not forsake them.
notes: God specializes in unfamiliar paths. When I don't recognize the way, that is not a sign I am lost — it may be a sign He is leading me somewhere new.

title: Jeremiah 29:11
message: For I know the plans I have for you, declares the LORD, plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.
notes: He already knows the plans. I am not waiting on God to figure it out — He is waiting for me to trust what He has already designed.

title: Proverbs 16:3
message: Commit to the LORD whatever you do, and he will establish your plans.
notes: The act of commitment activates His establishment. When I hand my plans to Him, He takes them and makes them stand.

title: Psalm 119:105
message: Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light on my path.
notes: A lamp for my feet — enough light for the next step, not necessarily the whole road. I walk by the light I have, trusting more will come.

title: Psalm 25:4-5
message: Show me your ways, LORD, teach me your paths. Guide me in your truth and teach me, for you are God my Savior, and my hope is in you all day long.
notes: David asks to be shown, taught, and guided — not just given an answer. I can ask God to show me the pattern of how He moves, not just what to do next.

title: Numbers 9:23
message: At the LORD's command they encamped, and at the LORD's command they set out. They obeyed the LORD's order, in accordance with his command through Moses.
notes: Israel moved only when God moved. Stillness was as much obedience as advance. I learn to wait for His signal before I move.

title: Acts 16:6-7
message: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the region of Phrygia and Galatia, having been kept by the Holy Spirit from preaching the word in the province of Asia. When they came to the border of Mysia, they tried to enter Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not allow them to.
notes: The Spirit redirected Paul away from good plans toward God's plans. A closed door is not always failure — sometimes it is precision guidance to something better.

title: Psalm 16:7
message: I will praise the LORD, who counsels me; even at night my heart instructs me.
notes: His counsel continues even while I sleep. Direction from God is not limited to my waking hours — He speaks in rest, in dreams, in quiet.

title: Isaiah 48:17
message: This is what the LORD says — your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: 'I am the LORD your God, who teaches you what is best for you, who directs you in the way you should go.'
notes: He teaches me what is best — not just what is good. God's direction is always toward the highest version of His purpose for my life.

title: Proverbs 11:14
message: For lack of guidance a nation falls, but victory is won through many advisers.
notes: God often speaks direction through counsel. I stay humble enough to receive His voice even when it comes through another person.

title: Psalm 31:3
message: Since you are my rock and my fortress, for the sake of your name lead me and guide me.
notes: God's reputation is tied to leading me well. When I ask Him to guide me, I am asking Him to demonstrate who He is through my life.

title: Luke 1:79
message: To shine on those living in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the path of peace.
notes: The destination of His guidance is peace. When I follow where God leads, I am always moving toward peace — not away from it.

title: Exodus 13:21
message: By day the LORD went ahead of them in a pillar of cloud to guide them on their way and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light, so that they could travel by day or night.
notes: He adapted His guidance to the conditions — cloud by day, fire by night. He meets me in whatever season or circumstance I am in and leads from there.

title: John 10:3-4
message: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out. When he has brought out all his own, he goes on ahead of them, and his sheep follow him because they know his voice.
notes: He calls me by name. His direction is never generic — it is personal, specific, and tailored to who I am and where I need to go.

title: Proverbs 4:11-12
message: I instruct you in the way of wisdom and lead you along straight paths. When you walk, your steps will not be hampered; when you run, you will not stumble.
notes: Walking in His direction removes friction. The path He leads me on is not obstructed — I can move with freedom, speed, and confidence.

title: 2 Chronicles 20:12
message: Our God, will you not judge them? For we have no power to face this vast army that is attacking us. We do not know what to do, but our eyes are on you.
notes: Jehoshaphat's honesty — 'we do not know what to do' — was the posture that unlocked direction. I can admit I don't know. That is where God begins to speak.

title: Psalm 23:3
message: He refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name's sake.
notes: He guides me for His name's sake — meaning His own glory is at stake in leading me well. I can trust He is invested in keeping me on the right path.
